revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:氷川神社 (板橋区氷川町)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Hikawa Jinja (氷川神社) | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}} {{ill|Itabashi|en|Itabashi|qid=Q232635|lt=Itabashi Ward|1=Itabashi}} {{ill|Hikawa Town|ja|氷川町 (板橋区)|lt=Hikawa Town|lt_ja=氷川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Town|1=Hikawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11549601}} | Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|qid=Q505469|lt=Inadahime|1=Kushinadahime}} | Rank = Local Shrine (郷社) | Founded = {{ill|1206|en|1206|qid=Q5230|lt=1206 AD|1=1206}}頃 | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Ryuzō Construction|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual Festival = {{ill|September 10|en|September 10|qid=Q2853|lt=September 10|1=September 10}} | Rituals = | image = [[File:Hikawa jinja hikawacho itabashi.JPG|250px|氷川神社 (板橋区氷川町)]] }}
'''Hikawa Jinja''' (氷川神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}} {{ill|Itabashi|en|Itabashi|qid=Q232635|lt=Itabashi Ward|1=Itabashi}}, in the area of {{ill|Hikawa Town|ja|氷川町 (板橋区)|lt=Hikawa Town|lt_ja=氷川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Town|1=Hikawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11549601}}.<ref>角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典13 東京都』 {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|en|Kadokawa Shoten|qid=Q1054765|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1978, 796頁</ref> It currently serves 19 towns as its parish area. The shrine buildings are constructed with reinforced concrete in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Ryuzō Construction|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style. The affiliated temple, {{ill|Bettō-ji Temple|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Bettō-ji|qid=Q11396755}}, is part of the {{ill|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|en|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|qid=Q3077351|lt=Toyosan Shingon|1=Shingon-shū Buzan-ha}} sect, known as Kanmeiji.
== Overview == {{ill|1206|en|1206|qid=Q5230|lt=1206 AD|1=1206}} ({{ill|Genkyū|en|Genkyū|qid=Q1194276|lt=Genkyū|1=Genkyū}} 3rd year ) around the time when {{ill|Toshima clan|en|Toshima clan|qid=Q2408600|lt=Toshima clan|1=Toshima clan}} – represented by {{ill|Toshima Kyotai|ja|豊島経泰|lt=Toshima Saemon-no-jō Kyotai|lt_ja=豊島左衛門尉経泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshima Kyotai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – who once held sway over the area, established the shrine by enshrining the branch spirits of {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|qid=Q505469|lt=Inadahime|1=Kushinadahime}} from the Hikawa Jinja of {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}} {{ill|Saitama (city)|en|Saitama (city)|qid=Q134635|lt=Saitama City|1=Saitama (city)}}. The location, chosen for its scenic setting along the {{ill|Shakujii River|en|Shakujii River|qid=Q7487093|lt=Shakujii River|1=Shakujii River}}, was favored as a site. Even after the decline of the Toshima clan, the local populace continued to revere the shrine as the guardian of {{ill|Itabashi-shuku|en|Itabashi-shuku|qid=Q3155832|lt=Itabashi-shuku|1=Itabashi-shuku}}, their chinju (local tutelary deity). However, a fire in {{ill|1889|en|1889|qid=Q7831|lt=1889 AD|1=1889}} (Meiji 22) destroyed the shrine and with it, the historical records that attested to its origins.
Rebuilt the year following the 1889 fire, the shrine was again completely destroyed in an {{ill|Airstrike|en|Airstrike|qid=Q2380335|lt=Air Raid|1=Airstrike}} in May {{ill|1945|en|1945|qid=Q5240|lt=1945 AD|1=1945}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed under the auspices of the Memorial Construction Support Association formed in {{ill|1954|en|1954|qid=Q18581|lt=1954 AD|1=1954}}, and was completed in September {{ill|1958|en|1958|qid=Q5253|lt=1958 AD|1=1958}}.
It was designated as a village shrine in {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=1872 AD|1=1872}} and as a local shrine in {{ill|1873|en|1873|qid=Q7757|lt=1873 AD|1=1873}}.
== Festivals == * Annual Festival – {{ill|September 10|en|September 10|qid=Q2853|lt=September 10|1=September 10}} * {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|qid=Q746798|lt=Setsubun Festival|1=Setsubun}} – {{ill|February 3|en|February 3|qid=Q2315|lt=February 3|1=February 3}} * {{ill|Obisha|ja|オビシャ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A ritual using bows and arrows to divine fortune and forecast agricultural harvests, common in the Kanto region. This rite was practiced at this shrine until around {{ill|1938|en|1938|qid=Q18645|lt=1938 AD|1=1938}}, but it has since been discontinued.
== Sub-shrines == * {{ill|Omitake Shrine|ja|小御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omitake Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Deity: {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|qid=Q8191705|lt=Iwanaga Hime|1=Iwanaga-hime}}) * {{ill|Tenso Shrine|ja|天祖神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社}} (Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}・{{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}・{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|qid=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine|1=Sengen shrine}} (Deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohananosakuyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} (Deity: {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|qid=Q2280323|lt=Hojokudaimyojin|1=Ukemochi}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}} (Deity: {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}) * {{ill|Tanishima Shrine|ja|淡島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanishima Shrine|1=Awashima Shrine (淡島神社)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65272459}} (Deity: {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|qid=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} (Deity: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima Hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}) * {{ill|Mitsumine Shrine|ja|三峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsumine Shrine|qq=}} (Deities: {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|1=Izanagi}}・{{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|1=Izanami}}) * {{ill|Komine Shrine|ja|古峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komine Shrine|1=Furumine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q106697652}} (Deity: {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyama-kizukami|1=Oyamakui no Kami}})
== Cultural Properties == * A hanging scroll of the shrine’s inscription penned by {{ill|Yamaoka Tesshū|en|Yamaoka Tesshū|qid=Q1158894|lt=Yamaoka Tesshū|1=Yamaoka Tesshū}} ({{ill|1892|en|1892|qid=Q7839|lt=1892 AD|1=1892}}) * {{ill|Fujizuka|en|Fujizuka|qid=Q5507555|lt=Fujizuka|1=Fujizuka}} – Registered as a cultural property by the ward on March 27, 2013. It is regarded as a valuable historical site for the study of local faith, folklore, and the activities of the Fuji Mountain Pilgrimage Society in the area. It is the only structure within the ward that was built during the Edo period ({{ill|1855|en|1855|qid=Q7673|lt=1855 AD|1=1855}}), and a monument to {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime|ja|木花咲夜姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands atop its summit. This Fujizuka was established by Nagata Chōshirō, a disciple of {{ill|Shokugyōshinroku|ja|食行身禄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokugyōshinroku|1=Jikigyō Miroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11666087}}, and for over 100 years, mountain-climbing rituals conducted by the Nagata Society – which bore his name – were held. The Nagata Society ceased its activities after {{ill|1966|en|1966|qid=Q2649|lt=1966 AD|1=1966}}, and its equipment was donated to the {{ill|Itabashi Local History Museum|ja|板橋区立郷土資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itabashi Local History Museum|1=Itabashi Historical Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q104696866}}.
== Parish Area == * {{ill|Itabashi|ja|板橋 (板橋区)|lt=Itabashi|lt_ja=板橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itabashi|1=Itabashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11532639}}: 1-chōme, part of 2-chōme, and 3–4 chōme * {{ill|Nakayado|ja|仲宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayado|1=Nakajuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378502}} * {{ill|Hikawa Town|ja|氷川町 (板橋区)|lt=Hikawa Town|lt_ja=氷川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Town|1=Hikawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11549601}} * {{ill|Naka-Itabashi|ja|中板橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-Itabashi|1=Naka-Itabashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11366189}} * {{ill|Sakae Town|ja|栄町 (板橋区)|lt=Sakae|lt_ja=栄町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae Town|1=Sakaechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11535718}} * {{ill|Naka Town|ja|仲町 (板橋区)|lt=Naka Town|lt_ja=仲町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Town|1=Nakachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378577}} * {{ill|Ōyama Nishi Town|ja|大山西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Nishi Town|1=Ōyama-Nishi-Chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11434696}} * Part of {{ill|Saiwai Town|ja|幸町 (板橋区)|lt=Saiwai|lt_ja=幸町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saiwai Town|1=Saiwaichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11484036}} * {{ill|Ōyama Town|ja|大山町 (板橋区)|lt=Ōyama|lt_ja=大山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Town|1=Ōyamachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11434659}} * {{ill|Ōyama Higashi Town|ja|大山東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Higashi Town|1=Ōyama-Higashi-Chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11434643}} * {{ill|Kaga|ja|加賀 (板橋区)|lt=Kaga|lt_ja=加賀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga|1=Kaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11399634}}: 1–2 chōme
== Sources == {{Reference Help}} <references/>
== Bibliography == {{Insufficient Citations|date=August 2020|section=1}} * "Itabashi Shrines" – Itabashi Ward Board of Education, 1981 * "Itabashi Ward History: Materials Vol. 5 Folk Customs" – Itabashi Ward, 1997 * "Itabashi Local History Encyclopedia" – Itabashi History Society, 1989 * "Explorations of Itabashi Historical Sites" – Itabashi Ward Board of Education, 1981
== External Links == * [https://hikawa-jinja.sakura.ne.jp Itabashi Nakayado Hikawa Jinja] (Official) * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/21_itabashi/21001.html Tokyo Shrine Agency: Hikawa Jinja]
